stance: default-guilty adversarial re-audit; LS reconstructed from scratch, not trusting audited numbers
reproduction_check: PASS - independent rebuild matches audited daily-rebal LS net within rounding (momentum negative all horizons; sentiment positive only f1/f5; f5 sentiment +49.855 net; residual spearman f1 -0.0776 / f2 -0.0697 / f3 +0.0098 / f5 +0.1671 reproduced exactly)
verdict_meaning: The ORIGINAL FINDING (is_tradeable_signal=false; sentiment cannot prove incremental alpha over free momentum; only f5 fires and it is single-name + right-tail driven; exploratory with no statistical power) is itself CONFIRMED and arguably strengthened by this adversarial pass. The thing that is 'weakened' is any temptation to read f5 as a real signal: it does NOT survive the falsification battery as tradeable alpha. So as a candidate alpha it is weakened-to-refuted; as a skeptical conclusion it holds.

a_momentum_disguise.free_momentum_is_not_alpha: momentum_20d_pct single-factor LS net is NEGATIVE every horizon (f1 -8.26 sum / f2 -15.63 / f3 -17.27 / f5 -2.27). High-momentum names underperform in-sample.
a_momentum_disguise.sentiment_steals_momentum: pearson(sentiment,momentum)=+0.524, pearson(sentiment,tech)=+0.560, so raw sentiment LS partially borrows the cross-section. Must residualize.
a_momentum_disguise.conclusion: 3 of 4 horizons show wrong-sign or near-zero incremental sentiment. Only f5 is positive. Momentum-controlled double sort is NEGATIVE for f1/f2/f3 and only positive at f5. No stable incremental edge.

b_single_day_single_name.conclusion: DECISIVE. Removing the single name MRVL cuts the f5 edge by ~69% (49.9->15.4). MRVL's contribution is itself two extreme right-tail LONG days (+58.5%, +39.3%). Edge is single-name + right-tail driven, exactly as the audit claimed.

c_overlap_effective_n.conclusion: f5 daily-rebal n=14 is heavily overlapping (5d holds). Non-overlapping gives only 3 independent rebalances. Autocorr-adjusted N_eff ~7-9 but the non-overlap reality is ~3. Any p-value is exploratory; CI is meaninglessly wide. Confirms audit's 'effective N ~3-4'.

d_survivorship_tradeability.note: Panel is constructed-restricted to 30 perp-mappable tickers; dropped names have no perp price so cannot be added back - this is an UNFALSIFIABLE-from-data limitation, flag it.
d_survivorship_tradeability.universe_regime_shift: 11 names early -> 22-30 names late; f5 only has 14 realized days and the 3 late days are unrealized for f5.
d_survivorship_tradeability.cross_sectional_dispersion: median within-day std of f1 = 3.187%; single-theme (AI/semis) cluster -> low dispersion makes the tercile spread fragile.
d_survivorship_tradeability.conclusion: f5 edge is concentrated in the EARLY regime (small 11-name universe) and shrinks late. Single-theme cluster means the long/short is not really diversified. Tradeability/survivorship bias is real and cannot be ruled out from this panel.

e_cost_stress.conclusion: Cost is NOT what kills it. Longer horizons (f5) incur cost only once per 5d hold, so the gross/net gap is tiny (~2.8 over 14 days). f5 and combined survive 0.20%/leg comfortably. f2/f3 sentiment die at 0.20 but they were already weak/negative. Cost is not the binding constraint - the binding constraint is single-name concentration + tiny N.

permutation_daily_rebal.multiple_comparison: 4 horizons x 2 score families = 8 tests; only f5 family passes p<0.05. With ~3 independent f5 rebalances and an obvious single-name driver, the low p is a small-sample artifact, not evidence of tradeable alpha.
